user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: platformio.ini
## My request for Codex:
In `DiskStats`, add a check to see if a file `firmware.bin` exists when all the other statistics are refreshed.
In `lib\MicroSdCard` make a file `FirmwareUpdate.cpp` and a corresponding header file. It should have one public function `update_firmware()`

`update_firmware()` has an optional callback that is a progress report about every 500ms or longer, the callback has bytes-written and bytes-total. Do not bother to check if 500ms has passed until every 4096 bytes written. And make sure the WDT doesn’t trip inside it.
Make a `FirmwareUpdateView.cpp` and corresponding header inside `lib\FlyGuiViews`
It uses `sprite_fwupdate` in the top center of the view, avoiding the top bar.
There are two buttons, one bottom left and one bottom right, also tied to `BtnA` and `BtnC`
The bottom left button is the ok-go button, using `sprite_greencheckmark_50`
The bottom right button is the cancel button, using `sprite_canceldoor_50`
On boot, after `MicroSdCard` initializes successfully, before any bluetooth or wifi initialization
If the file `firmware.bin` appears on the card, show this dialog.

If the battery is not full-ish, do not show the go button, instead, show a message “To perform a firmware update, battery needs to be fully charged”. Otherwise, make it say “Do you want to update firmware?”

When the user clicks go, remove the cancel button, remove the go button, replace it with `sprite_hourglass_60_1`. Start the firmware update by calling `update_firmware()` (and registering the progress callback)
Inside that function, open the `firmware.bin` file, write it into the ota buffer/inactive partition.

The progress call back triggers a cycling of the hourglass graphic through `sprite_hourglass_60_1 sprite_hourglass_60_2 sprite_hourglass_60_3` and also please use code very similar to `void CloudUploadView::drawProgress()` and the new `lib\FlyGui\ProgressBar.cpp` to draw a progress update.

All of this needs to be done in the same thread, no servicing any other polls or pumps, not even the gui itself. We only need the draw calls for the hourglass, and that’s only two instances. The clock can appear frozen. The battery tracker and `Hotel` needs to not do anything at all.

When fully written, we check if it is valid using internal ota mechanisms, return from `update_firmware()` , with some sort of return code about how successful it is (if secure boot is on, this will verify if it is valid). if it is successful, mark the partition as the boot partition, and show the user a thumbs up `sprite_thumbsup_100` up top and confirmation message, and delete `firmware.bin` from the microSD card. Otherwise, use `sprite_xcircle_100` and say it failed. Any touch on the screen reboots.

As a rule, `lib\FlyGuiViews\FirmwareUpdateView.cpp` should not reference any internal ESP OTA functionality. And `lib\MicroSdCard\FirmwareUpdate.cpp` should avoid referencing any GUI related calls, as that should be handled by the callback and the return code.
agent_reply: - Added [FirmwareUpdate.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/FirmwareUpdate.cpp:36) / header with synchronous `update_firmware()` using ESP OTA APIs, 4096-byte write chunks, ~500ms progress callback checks, and WDT feeding.

- Added [FirmwareUpdateView.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FirmwareUpdateView.cpp:53) / header with the prompt, battery gate, progress bar/hourglass cycling, success/failure result screens, and touch-to-reboot.
- Wired the view into boot before Wi-Fi/Bluetooth setup in [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:417).
- Plain `pio run` still fails because the default `thefly` env does not define `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL`; that appears pre-existing.
Note: `src/version.c` was already dirty before I started and remains dirty after the version-generation build script ran.

- Added [FirmwareUpdate.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/FirmwareUpdate.cpp:36) / header with synchronous `update_firmware()` using ESP OTA APIs, 4096-byte write chunks, ~500ms progress callback checks, and WDT feeding.

- Added [FirmwareUpdateView.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FirmwareUpdateView.cpp:53) / header with the prompt, battery gate, progress bar/hourglass cycling, success/failure result screens, and touch-to-reboot.
- Wired the view into boot before Wi-Fi/Bluetooth setup in [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:417).
- Plain `pio run` still fails because the default `thefly` env does not define `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL`; that appears pre-existing.
Note: `src/version.c` was already dirty before I started and remains dirty after the version-generation build script ran.

commit_message: diagnostics, fixed a bug with speaker peak decay
